title: Add characterization tests for Episode/Numeric introspection and results fixtures
description:
Before making behavior changes in conceptql-grt (Episode introspection) and conceptql-n61 (Numeric as_criterion), we need tests that lock in current behavior. Anything untested will break silently.

Gaps identified:
- Zero unit tests for events_per_patient, multiple_vocabularies, vocabularies on ANY operator (methods were added in ffb6593b and 419adbdb without tests)
- Episode has one SQL-level unit test file but no behavior tests
- Numeric has no unit test file at all
- Numeric as_criterion (no upstream) has no results_* fixture — existing num_values_* fixtures only select (criterion_domain, value_as_number), not column_family or other default columns

Tasks:

1. Add characterization tests to test/lib/conceptql/operators/episode_test.rb:
   - episode(icd9).events_per_patient == :multiple (inherits from upstream)
   - episode(first(icd9)).events_per_patient == :single (inherits from single upstream)
   - episode(icd9).multiple_vocabularies == false (current behavior)
   - union(episode(icd9), episode(cpt)).multiple_vocabularies == true (CURRENT behavior via vocabularies walking through)
   - episode(icd9).operator.vocabularies != [] (CURRENT behavior — walks upstream)

2. Create test/lib/conceptql/operators/numeric_test.rb with characterization tests:
   - numeric(1).events_per_patient == :multiple (CURRENT leaf default)
   - numeric(1).multiple_vocabularies == false
   - numeric(1, icd9).events_per_patient inherits from upstream
   - numeric(1, icd9).multiple_vocabularies inherits from upstream

3. Create test/statements/numeric/results_as_criterion.json with statement ["numeric", 1] to exercise the full results path including column_family. Generate expected fixture via CONCEPTQL_OVERWRITE_TEST_RESULTS. This will empirically verify whether column_family comes out as 'default' or NULL in the no-upstream path.

All tests must pass against current code. The conceptql-grt and conceptql-n61 work will UPDATE specific expected values in these tests as part of their behavior changes — but the bulk of these tests act as regression coverage to catch unintended ripple effects.

Acceptance:
- test/lib/conceptql/operators/episode_test.rb has new introspection describe block with passing tests
- test/lib/conceptql/operators/numeric_test.rb exists with passing tests
- test/statements/numeric/results_as_criterion.json exists with generated expected fixture
- Full test suite passes (3 matrix configs)
- Fixture reveals whether numeric as_criterion column_family is 'default' or NULL
close_reason: Added characterization tests for Episode (introspection methods including variadic cross-cuts) and Numeric (introspection methods + SQL-level column_family bug pinning). Empirically confirmed Numeric as_criterion produces NULL column_family — n61 fix needs to address this. All 3 matrix configs pass with 452 runs each. Tessa-audited (B- with the highest-leverage improvements applied).

title: Add multiple_vocabularies introspection method to operators
description:
Add a heritable multiple_vocabularies method (returns true/false) and a vocabularies helper method.

Files to modify and exact changes:

1. lib/conceptql/operators/operator.rb — add two instance methods:
   - vocabularies: if respond_to?(:vocab_entry) return [vocab_entry.omopv5_vocabulary_id], elsif upstreams.any? return upstreams.flat_map(&:vocabularies).uniq, else return []
   - multiple_vocabularies: if upstreams.any? then upstreams.first.multiple_vocabularies else false end

2. lib/conceptql/operators/binary_operator_operator.rb — override: left.multiple_vocabularies

3. lib/conceptql/operators/union.rb — override: return true if upstreams.any?(&:multiple_vocabularies); vocabularies.length > 1

4. lib/conceptql/operators/co_reported.rb — same logic as Union

5. lib/conceptql/operators/concurrent_within.rb — same logic as Union

6. lib/conceptql/operators/sum.rb — same logic as Union (Sum groups by source_vocabulary_id so it preserves vocabulary distinctions)

7. lib/conceptql/query.rb — add public method: def multiple_vocabularies; operator.multiple_vocabularies; end

The vocabularies helper walks the tree to collect vocabulary IDs from selection operators, so variadic operators correctly detect different vocabularies even when their immediate children are not Vocabulary operators (e.g., Union of two During operators wrapping different vocabularies).

multiple_vocabularies is purely an introspection method. It is NOT added to Scope or to any SQL output columns.

This adds new methods only — does not change query output structure.

title: Add events_per_patient introspection method to operators
description:
Add a heritable events_per_patient method that returns :single or :multiple.

Files to modify and exact changes:

1. Create lib/conceptql/behaviors/single_event.rb — module SingleEvent with def events_per_patient; :single; end
2. Create lib/conceptql/behaviors/multiple_event.rb — module MultipleEvent with def events_per_patient; :multiple; end
3. lib/conceptql/operators/operator.rb — add default instance method: delegates to upstreams.first, falls back to :multiple
4. lib/conceptql/operators/binary_operator_operator.rb — override: left.events_per_patient
5. lib/conceptql/operators/union.rb — override: :single only if all upstreams are :single, else :multiple
6. lib/conceptql/operators/co_reported.rb — same logic as Union
7. lib/conceptql/operators/concurrent_within.rb — same logic as Union
8. lib/conceptql/operators/sum.rb — override: always :multiple (Sum groups by many columns, not per-patient)
9. lib/conceptql/operators/occurrence.rb — include ConceptQL::Behaviors::SingleEvent (First and Last inherit from Occurrence, so they get it automatically)
10. lib/conceptql/operators/one_in_two_out.rb — include ConceptQL::Behaviors::SingleEvent
11. lib/conceptql/query.rb — add public method: def events_per_patient; operator.events_per_patient; end

These are the only 4 operators that guarantee one row per patient: Occurrence, First, Last, OneInTwoOut. No others.

This adds new methods only — does not change query output structure, so no fixture breakage beyond what CQL-1 already caused.
close_reason: Implemented and tested across all 3 CI matrix configs

title: Add column_family to DEFAULT_COLUMNS and set in all selection operators
description:
Add column_family as a :String to Scope::DEFAULT_COLUMNS (scope.rb line 20) so it appears on every query, like label.

Then set it via the :replace mechanism in Operator#select_it (operator.rb line 276). The exact mechanism:

1. Add a column_family method to base Operator returning nil.
2. Override in behavior mixins:
   - Utilizable (utilizable.rb): return 'inpatient'
   - Drugish (drugish.rb): return 'drug'
   - Labish (labish.rb): return 'lab'
   Note: Utilizable uses ActiveSupport::Concern with included block. Drugish/Labish use self.included(base) with base.define_method.
3. In select_it (operator.rb line 276), inject column_family alongside label:
   cf = column_family || (self.class.basic_type == :selection ? 'other' : nil)
   opts[:replace] ||= {}
   opts[:replace][:label] = label if label
   opts[:replace][:column_family] = cf if cf

This produces: Utilizable operators get 'inpatient', Drugish get 'drug', Labish get 'lab', other selection operators get 'other', non-selection operators let upstream value pass through.

CRITICAL: This changes the output of every query and will cause mass fixture test failures. After implementation, run the full test suite, then STOP. Generate a summary of failures (count, pattern, anything unexpected). Alert the human reviewer. Do NOT update fixture expectations — the reviewer will do that.
close_reason: Implemented column_family in DEFAULT_COLUMNS with behavior-specific values (default/inpatient/drug/lab), pass-through for non-table operators, and updated all fixtures

title: Fix: select_remove triggers columns\! on nonexistent temp tables
description: sort_extracted_ctes calls ds.sql on CTE datasets, which triggers select_remove -> columns\! -> DB query for temp tables that don't exist yet. Root cause: Window::Table#selected_columns can't see through from_self's AliasedExpression, so remove_window_id falls through to select_remove which eagerly introspects the DB. Fix: (1) make selected_columns unwrap AliasedExpression, (2) handle non-Symbol columns in remove_window_id so we never fall through to select_remove when column info is available.
close_reason: Fixed Window::Table#selected_columns to unwrap AliasedExpression from from_self, and extended remove_window_id to handle non-Symbol columns. This prevents select_remove from triggering eager columns! DB queries on temp tables that may not exist during sort_extracted_ctes.

title: Fix Dependabot security alert: json format string injection
description: High severity: Ruby JSON has a format string injection vulnerability (alert #22, package: json)
close_reason: Updated json gem from 2.19.2 to 2.19.3, all tests pass

title: Add pre-push CI gate running the local test suite
description:
Per Ryan's global CI-gate policy (decided 2026-05-08): private repos with billed CI minutes should have a pre-push git hook that runs the FULL local equivalent of CI, so remote CI becomes the last line of defense for cross-environment edge cases, not the first place a problem surfaces.

conceptql currently has NO pre-push CI gate — only the beads pre-push hook (core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks). main has branch protection requiring a "ci" status check, but pushes can bypass it via admin (observed 2026-06-04).

What CI enforces (.github/workflows/run_tests.yml): TESTS ONLY across 3 jobs — Run-PostgreSQL-Tests (matrix of data_model x search_path), Run-Spark-Tests (ruby 4.0 + Java + Spark/hexspace), Run-DuckDB-Tests (ruby 3.2 + downloaded duckdb test data). No rubocop/lint/security jobs. A final "ci" job gates on all three.

Local feasibility: PostgreSQL is fully reproducible locally via docker compose (test_data.ignitor image present) — this is the primary platform and the documented 3-config matrix (gdm_wide / gdm+ohdsi / gdm+gdm_vocabs). Spark needs cluster infra; DuckDB needs the test-data file. Decide which backends the gate runs locally vs. leaves to CI as cross-env coverage.
design:
Approach (follow code_set_catalog reference impl):
1. Add a CI-equivalent recipe (script or rake task, e.g. bin/ci) that runs the test path CI runs — at minimum the Postgres docker-compose matrix (all 3 configs), sequential + fail-fast. Reuse the 3 commands documented in conceptql/CLAUDE.md.
2. Wire a pre-push hook to call it. Integration with beads is the tricky part: beads owns core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks. Either (a) unset core.hooksPath, symlink the non-pre-commit beads hooks into .git/hooks, install pre-commit framework, add beads pre-commit + a stages:[pre-push] CI hook (the code_set_catalog pattern); or (b) a plain .git/hooks/pre-push that runs both bd hooks run pre-push AND bin/ci. Pick the lower-risk option that keeps beads working.
3. Bypass remains git push --no-verify for emergencies.

Open scope decision: include Spark + DuckDB locally (needs infra) or Postgres-only v1 with Spark/DuckDB left to CI (aligns with the policy's "CI catches cross-env edge cases"). Recommend Postgres-matrix v1.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented CI-equivalent recipe exists and runs the Postgres test matrix (3 configs) sequential + fail-fast.
- A pre-push hook invokes it; a failing test blocks the push; passing allows it.
- Beads hooks (pre-commit export, pre-push) still fire correctly — beads workflow not broken.
- git push --no-verify still works as the emergency bypass.
- Documented in conceptql/CLAUDE.md (and decision recorded).
close_reason: Pre-push gate implemented: 'just ci' runs the gdm_wide Postgres config (user chose gdm_wide-only for ~1.5min push latency; other 2 Postgres configs + Spark/DuckDB stay in remote CI as last line of defense). Appended to the git-tracked .beads/hooks/pre-push hook (repo-wide, integrates with beads). Root-cause lock churn fixed by pinning parallel<2.0 for the ruby 3.2 floor. Validated: gate passes 456/0, idempotent (no lock churn), hook syntax-checked. Documented in CLAUDE.md. Bypass: git push --no-verify.

title: Spark 3.3.2 mis-evaluates is_primary CASE in gdm_wide_it; primary_concept_id null-resolves
description:
## Summary

`Behaviors::Utilizable#gdm_wide_it` emits a Sequel `is_primary` CASE expression in a multi-WHEN form that Spark 3.3.2 mis-evaluates when its result is then consumed by an outer `CASE expr WHEN val THEN col ELSE NULL END`. Three columns end up NULL on rows where they should not: `primary_concept_id`, `source_value`, `source_vocabulary_id`. Postgres and DuckDB evaluate the same SQL correctly; only Spark 3.3.x is affected.

This is the immediate root cause of t_shank ci-test-spark failures in the `diabetic_boys_all_hospitalizations` fixture (vocabulation-ak1, now closed with diagnosis pointing here).

## Where it surfaces

t_shank, with `CONCEPTQL_DATA_MODEL=gdm_wide` (Spark-only path):

```
ci-test-spark failures (2):
  - diabetic_boys_all_hospitalizations_export-events
  - diabetic_boys_all_hospitalizations_export-vocabulary_summary
```

Both failures: expected output has rows with `primary_concept_id=44827629` (ICD9CM "Other and unspecified hyperlipidemia", source_value 2724); spark output has NULL in those three columns.

`ci-test-postgres` (uses `CONCEPTQL_DATA_MODEL=gdm`, the `gdm_it` path, NOT `gdm_wide_it`) passes 1480/0/7 — so this code path is exercised ONLY by Spark CI. There is currently no Postgres or DuckDB cross-check against `gdm_wide_it`.

## The bug

`lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:91-102` builds `is_primary` as:

```ruby
def gdm_wide_it(db, all_primary_ids, all_source_type_ids)
  # TODO: Reinstate the original, less clunky version of this change once
  # we stop supporting Spark 3.3.x
  primary_cases = {}
  all_primary_ids.each do |primary_id|
    primary_cases[{ Sequel[:provenance_concept_id] => primary_id }] = 1
  end

  # This generates some gnarly SQL if there are no primary cases (like
  # when we run a statement without a database to grab concepts from)
  is_primary = Sequel[0]
  is_primary = Sequel.case(primary_cases, 0) unless primary_cases.empty?
```

That emits:

```sql
(CASE WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 38000183) THEN 1
      WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 38000199) THEN 1
      WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 100000070) THEN 1
      WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 100012440) THEN 1
      ELSE 0 END) AS `is_primary`
```

Then at lines 125-127, the outer SELECT uses `is_primary` in a CASE-with-expression form:

```ruby
Sequel.case({ 1 => :clinical_code_concept_id }, nil, :is_primary).as(:primary_concept_id),
Sequel.case({ 1 => :clinical_code_source_value },  nil, :is_primary).as(:source_value),
Sequel.case({ 1 => :clinical_code_vocabulary_id }, nil, :is_primary).as(:source_vocabulary_id)
```

emitting:

```sql
(CASE `is_primary` WHEN 1 THEN `clinical_code_concept_id` ELSE NULL END) AS `primary_concept_id`
```

Both pieces are logically correct. But Spark 3.3.2 fails to recognize the matched WHEN in the inner CASE for at least some rows in the partition, so `is_primary` materializes as 0 everywhere, ROW_NUMBER picks a non-primary row as nummy=1, and the outer CASE null-resolves the three derived columns.

The pre-existing TODO comment at lines 92-93 already names Spark 3.3.x as the reason this code is "clunky." The current multi-WHEN form was itself a workaround for an earlier Spark 3.3 bug; we now have a different Spark 3.3 bug **with the workaround**.

## Evidence the data is fine and the logic is fine

The user already validated this independently (vocabulation-ak1 NOTES block):

- Spark Thrift directly: `descendants_of(100012440)` returns `[38000183, 38000199, 100000070, 100012440]` ✓
- The relevant `wide.observations` row for collection 37 has `provenance_concept_id=100000070` (a primary descendant) ✓
- A hand-rolled Spark CTE using `CASE WHEN provenance_concept_id IN (...) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END` (instead of multi-WHEN) gets `primary_concept_id=44827629` correctly ✓
- The exact SQL Sequel emits, replayed in DuckDB, returns the correct result ✓ (`vocabulation/claude_stuff/exact_pipeline.sql`)

So: data correct, logic correct, only the Spark + multi-WHEN combination fails.

## Cross-reference

- vocabulation-ak1 — origin of the failure investigation; closed with pointer here.
- The earlier 2026-05-19 vocab fix (gdm_vocabs ancestors restoration of the `100012440→100000070` edge) was real and necessary, but independent: it restored a precondition (`descendants_of(100012440)` must include 100000070), not the Spark codegen behavior.

## Files touched

- `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb` — gdm_wide_it method only.
design:
## Primary fix (required) — emit IN-form CASE for is_primary

In `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb`, replace lines 91-102 (the body of `gdm_wide_it` up through the `is_primary` assignment) with:

```ruby
def gdm_wide_it(db, all_primary_ids, all_source_type_ids)
  # Emit `CASE WHEN provenance_concept_id IN (...) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END`.
  # The previous multi-WHEN form (`Sequel.case({{prov=>id1}=>1, {prov=>id2}=>1, ...}, 0)`)
  # is mis-evaluated by Spark 3.3.2 when its result feeds the outer
  # `CASE is_primary WHEN 1 THEN col ELSE NULL END` below. See conceptql-pk7.
  is_primary =
    if all_primary_ids.empty?
      Sequel[0]
    else
      Sequel.case(
        [[{ Sequel[:provenance_concept_id] => all_primary_ids }, 1]],
        0
      )
    end
```

That renders as `(CASE WHEN (provenance_concept_id IN (38000183, 38000199, 100000070, 100012440)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)` — matching the user's hand-verified-working form in Spark Thrift.

Mechanism: `Sequel.case` with an array-of-pairs as the first argument and a hash inside (where the hash value is an array) routes through `from_value_pairs`, which emits `IN (...)` — see `sequel/lib/sequel/sql.rb:1214` (`case_expression_sql_append`).

Leave lines 104-130 (the rest of the method) unchanged. The outer CASE-with-expression form at lines 125-127 is consumed downstream and may also be at risk on Spark 3.3 in other places, but in this method it works correctly *once* `is_primary` materializes correctly. See "Optional secondary fix" below for defense in depth.

## Optional secondary fix (defense in depth) — rewrite outer CASE-with-expression

If validation (below) shows the primary fix alone resolves the Spark failure, this is not strictly necessary. If we still see issues, also rewrite `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:125-127` from CASE-with-expression to CASE-WHEN:

```ruby
Sequel.case([[{ Sequel[:is_primary] => 1 }, :clinical_code_concept_id]], nil).as(:primary_concept_id),
Sequel.case([[{ Sequel[:is_primary] => 1 }, :clinical_code_source_value ]], nil).as(:source_value),
Sequel.case([[{ Sequel[:is_primary] => 1 }, :clinical_code_vocabulary_id]], nil).as(:source_vocabulary_id)
```

This switches from `CASE \`is_primary\` WHEN 1 THEN col ELSE NULL END` to `CASE WHEN (\`is_primary\` = 1) THEN col ELSE NULL END`. Spark 3.3 has multiple known CASE-with-expression evaluation oddities; CASE-WHEN is the better-trodden Catalyst path.

## Do NOT do (rejected alternative)

Rewriting `gdm_wide_it` to use a separate `primary_observations` LEFT JOIN (mirroring `gdm_it` at lines 42-89). It's the architecturally cleanest fix, but it's a larger refactor and removes the CASE dependency entirely. Hold this as a fallback only if both fixes above fail. Open a separate refactor bead if we want to pursue it long-term.

## Validation plan

### Step A — Capture the actual emitted SQL (cheap sanity check before code change)

In t_shank, the test harness already wires Sequel SQL logging to `log/rspec.log` via `loggers: [logger]` at `spec/support/exports/all_exports_spec_manager.rb:151`. Run only the failing example:

```bash
cd ~/projects/outins/t_shank
TSHANK_SYNPUF_HOST_PATH=/tmp/spark_on_docker/synpuf_test_data just ci-test-spark \
  -e 'diabetic_boys_all_hospitalizations'
grep -A2 -B1 'is_primary' log/rspec.log | head -80
```

Expected: confirms the multi-WHEN form is what Spark receives, matching what the postgres-mock Sequel probe predicted (vocabulation/claude_stuff/probe_sequel_basic.rb → vocabulation/claude_stuff/exact_pipeline.sql).

### Step B — Reproduce in Spark Thrift in isolation (optional but high signal)

Against `hexspace://172.17.0.1:10000/default` or any spark-sql REPL against Spark 3.3.2:

Query A (multi-WHEN, predicted broken):
```sql
WITH src AS (
  SELECT * FROM `public`.`observations` WHERE admission_detail_id = 37
),
flagged AS (
  SELECT *, (CASE WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 38000183) THEN 1
                  WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 38000199) THEN 1
                  WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 100000070) THEN 1
                  WHEN (`provenance_concept_id` = 100012440) THEN 1
                  ELSE 0 END) AS `is_primary` FROM src
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY `admission_detail_id`
    ORDER BY `is_primary` DESC, `collection_id`, `clinical_code_concept_id`) AS `nummy` FROM flagged
)
SELECT `is_primary`,
       (CASE `is_primary` WHEN 1 THEN `clinical_code_concept_id` ELSE NULL END) AS primary_concept_id,
       `clinical_code_concept_id`, `clinical_code_source_value`, `provenance_concept_id`
FROM ranked WHERE `nummy` = 1;
```

Query B (IN form, predicted working — matches the proposed fix):
```sql
WITH src AS (
  SELECT * FROM `public`.`observations` WHERE admission_detail_id = 37
),
flagged AS (
  SELECT *, (CASE WHEN `provenance_concept_id` IN (38000183, 38000199, 100000070, 100012440) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS `is_primary` FROM src
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY `admission_detail_id`
    ORDER BY `is_primary` DESC, `collection_id`, `clinical_code_concept_id`) AS `nummy` FROM flagged
)
SELECT `is_primary`,
       (CASE `is_primary` WHEN 1 THEN `clinical_code_concept_id` ELSE NULL END) AS primary_concept_id,
       `clinical_code_concept_id`, `clinical_code_source_value`, `provenance_concept_id`
FROM ranked WHERE `nummy` = 1;
```

Expected:
- A: nummy=1 row is `38000499 / DRG / 201`, is_primary=0, primary_concept_id=NULL → reproduces the bug.
- B: nummy=1 row is `44827629 / ICD9CM / 2724`, is_primary=1, primary_concept_id=44827629 → matches the fixture expectation.

If both queries give the WORKING result (i.e., Step B cannot reproduce in isolation), the trigger is at a deeper nesting level — wrap Query A in two extra `SELECT * FROM (...)` layers and retest. Conceptql wraps `gdm_wide_it` in additional `from_self` calls (lines 107, 110, 115, 117, 129) plus left joins, so the actual emitted SQL has more nesting than my minimal reproduction.

### Step C — Test the fix end-to-end

After applying the primary fix:

```bash
# In conceptql gem:
cd ~/projects/outins/jigsaw/main/gems/conceptql
bundle exec rspec  # if conceptql has its own tests touching gdm_wide_it; check first

# In t_shank, bump the conceptql revision in Gemfile.lock to the fix commit:
cd ~/projects/outins/t_shank
# (bundle update conceptql / bundle install)
TSHANK_SYNPUF_HOST_PATH=/tmp/spark_on_docker/synpuf_test_data just ci-test-spark \
  -e 'diabetic_boys_all_hospitalizations'
```

Expected: 0 failures on the diabetic_boys_all_hospitalizations fixture. Then run the full Spark suite — target is 1480 examples / 0 failures / 8 pending (matching Postgres + 1 for the now-working spark spec).

Per project rule for actually running tests: delegate test execution to Runa.

## Risk assessment

- **Blast radius is small.** `Sequel.case` with multi-WHEN appears in conceptql only in `gdm_wide_it`. Other `Sequel.case` usages use the two-arg hash form which renders to standalone WHEN clauses and is unaffected. Grep to confirm: `grep -rn 'Sequel\.case' lib/conceptql/`.
- **No Postgres-with-gdm_wide test exists.** Postgres CI uses `CONCEPTQL_DATA_MODEL=gdm` (the `gdm_it` path). After this fix lands, there's no second backend cross-checking gdm_wide_it. Consider adding a DuckDB gdm_wide test in a follow-up bead (DuckDB test infrastructure already exists; see `t_shank/spec/support/exports/all_exports_spec_manager.rb:81-109`).
- **Spark 3.3 is end-of-life-adjacent.** When `SPARK_VERSION` moves off 3.3 (currently pinned at 3.3.2 in `t_shank/.github/workflows/main.yml:52`), the TODO at `utilizable.rb:92-93` becomes resolvable: the older "less clunky" form referenced by the comment can be restored. The IN-form proposed here is functionally equivalent and likely fine to leave in place even on newer Spark.
- **Cross-repo coordination.** The fix is in conceptql gem; it's consumed by t_shank (Gemfile.lock pin) and potentially jigsaw-diagram-editor. Bump t_shank first (smaller blast radius); coordinate with jigsaw-diagram-editor before they bump.

## Artifacts from the investigation (in vocabulation/claude_stuff/)

- `probe_sequel_basic.rb` — Sequel postgres-mock probe that prints the literal SQL gdm_wide_it emits.
- `exact_pipeline.sql` — the literal Sequel-emitted SQL, replayed in DuckDB (returns correct result, confirming logic is fine).
- `simulate_gdm_wide_it.sql`, `simulate_full.sql`, `probe_obs.sql`, `test_subquery_join.sql` — DuckDB simulations and observations parquet probes.

## Key file/line citations

- `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:91-130` — gdm_wide_it method.
- `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:92-93` — pre-existing Spark 3.3.x TODO.
- `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:94-102` — is_primary CASE construction (PRIMARY FIX SITE).
- `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:125-127` — outer CASE-with-expression (optional secondary fix site).
- `lib/conceptql/behaviors/utilizable.rb:42-89` — gdm_it path used by Postgres; reference architecture for the LEFT JOIN refactor alternative.
- `lib/conceptql/rdbms/spark.rb` — Spark adapter (no CASE override; uses default Sequel rendering).
- t_shank refs (out of this repo, for context): `lib/t_shank/rdbms_adapter/spark.rb`, `spec/support/exports/all_exports_spec_manager.rb:151`, `.github/workflows/main.yml:52`, `justfile:66-84`.
notes:
Hypothesis falsified 2026-05-22. The outer CASE-with-expression at utilizable.rb:127-129 is NOT the bug. In-harness spec (spark_case_form_verification_spec.rb in t_shank) generated the SQL, patched 3 outer CASE-with-expression forms to CASE-WHEN form, ran both against Spark — BOTH returned identical row for collection_id=37/person_id=2 with primary_concept_id=NULL.

New lead: the INNER is_primary CASE on Spark only has 4 primary-descendant IDs in IN list (38000183, 100012440, 38000199, 100000070) where the postgres lexicon dump showed 8 (also 38000248, 38000250, 38000266, 38000268). Lexicon descendants may have shifted, OR the row's provenance_concept_id is in one of the 4 missing IDs, making is_primary=0 on Spark. Next: inspect observations for person_id=2 to find actual provenance_concept_id and determine real root cause.

title: Numeric as_criterion: fix events_per_patient and add results test coverage
description:
When Numeric is used without an upstream (as_criterion path, SELECT value_as_number FROM patients), it produces exactly one row per patient. Structurally this is events_per_patient=:single, but it currently returns :multiple via the default leaf-operator fallback.

Also, no existing results_* fixture exercises the full column_family / default-columns output path for Numeric as_criterion. The num_values_* tests use NumericValuesResults which only selects (criterion_domain, value_as_number) — they would not catch a NULL column_family or other default-column bug.

Investigation during column_family work suggests column_family IS correctly set to 'default' via the double-selectify (inner as_criterion call sets NULL via domain:person, then evaluate wraps with select_it which sets 'default' via the specific_table path). But this has never been empirically verified with a fixture.

Tasks:
1. Override events_per_patient on Numeric (or just as_criterion path) to return :single when stream is nil
2. Add a results_* fixture for Numeric with no upstream, e.g. ["numeric", 1]
3. Verify the fixture output has column_family='default' (not NULL) — if NULL, fix the root cause
4. Verify multiple_vocabularies on the fixture is false (should already be correct)

Acceptance:
- New results_* fixture exists for Numeric as_criterion
- Fixture output rows all have column_family='default' (no NULLs)
- Numeric with no upstream reports events_per_patient=:single
- Numeric with no upstream reports multiple_vocabularies=false
close_reason: Fixes landed in commits 57ef4aa5 (grt) and 30adb0c4 (n61). Episode now overrides vocabularies/multiple_vocabularies to reflect that it strips source_vocabulary_id; Numeric now overrides events_per_patient for the as_criterion path and explicitly sets column_family=DEFAULT in as_criterion. Characterization tests flipped, results_no_upstream.json fixture updated. All 3 CI matrix configs pass (454 runs, 1133 assertions, 0 failures).

title: Fix Episode introspection: override vocabularies and multiple_vocabularies
description:
Episode strips source_vocabulary_id from its output (sets NULL), so rows from an Episode do not carry upstream vocabulary information. Downstream consumers can't distinguish vocabularies in the data.

But the current introspection methods walk the upstream tree. If an Episode is built from icd9 (or a union of icd9+cpt), multiple_vocabularies reports based on what was upstream, not on what Episode actually emits.

Example problem:
  union(episode(icd9), episode(cpt))
  - Both episodes return multiple_vocabularies=false (false already at leaf level)
  - But Union falls through to vocabularies.length>1, which walks the tree and finds ['ICD9CM','CPT4']
  - Union reports multiple_vocabularies=true
  - But actual output has NULL source_vocabulary_id on every row — misleading

Fix: Episode should override BOTH methods to reflect that it strips vocabulary metadata.

  def multiple_vocabularies
    false
  end

  def vocabularies
    []
  end

Acceptance:
- Episode.multiple_vocabularies returns false regardless of upstream
- Episode.vocabularies returns [] regardless of upstream
- union(episode(icd9), episode(cpt)).multiple_vocabularies returns false (because vocabularies is empty from both)
- Test coverage for both methods via a new fixture
close_reason: Fixes landed in commits 57ef4aa5 (grt) and 30adb0c4 (n61). Episode now overrides vocabularies/multiple_vocabularies to reflect that it strips source_vocabulary_id; Numeric now overrides events_per_patient for the as_criterion path and explicitly sets column_family=DEFAULT in as_criterion. Characterization tests flipped, results_no_upstream.json fixture updated. All 3 CI matrix configs pass (454 runs, 1133 assertions, 0 failures).

title: Migrate select_remove call sites to smart_select_remove
description: After sequelizer-fkd lands the smart_select_remove extension, update conceptql to use it. Remove the manual selected_columns/outer_column_name workaround in Window::Table and let the extension handle it. Call sites: temporal_operator.rb:100, occurrence.rb:71, window/table.rb:71 fallback, statement_tests.rb:175. This should eliminate the remaining 10 pre-existing test errors (5x l.person_id not found from temporal_operator, 5x admission_date from hospice/hospitalization/snf).
close_reason: Migrated to smart_select_remove extension. 426 runs, 0 failures, 0 errors — all 10 pre-existing errors eliminated.

title: Hybrid remove_window_id: support both mock and DuckDB-strict scoping
description:
remove_window_id currently uses ds.opts[:select] with select_remove fallback (46f70ca9), which broke mock-based unit tests because select_remove requires the :select_remove Sequel extension. The previous approach (681830bc) used selected_columns(ds) to look through from_self, but that produced inner-scope expressions that DuckDB rejected.

Fix: when opts[:select] is nil (from_self datasets), use selected_columns to discover inner column expressions, then map them to safe outer-scope symbol names (extract alias/name only). This avoids both the DuckDB strict-scoping issue and the select_remove dependency in test environments. Keep select_remove as ultimate fallback when selected_columns returns nil.

Also fix the 5 failing Window::Table unit tests that were written for the selected_columns approach but broken by 46f70ca9.
close_reason: Implemented hybrid three-tier strategy in remove_window_id: direct opts[:select], selected_columns→outer symbols, select_remove fallback. All 15 Window::Table tests pass.

title: Consider CPT4_HCPCS merged-vocabulary integration (retire cpt_or_hcpcs from exposure; re-evaluate format regexps)
description: Gate for ConceptQL adoption of the upstream CPT4_HCPCS merged vocabulary (built in ohdsi_supplemental_vocabs). See conceptql seed 'CPT4_HCPCS merged vocabulary — ConceptQL integration (deferred)'. Decided direction: retire the cpt_or_hcpcs operator from exposure but keep it functional for legacy statements. Open items to decide+implement: (1) whether CPT4_HCPCS goes in config/multiple_vocabularies.csv vs plain vocabulary_id lookup; (2) re-evaluate/fix format_regexp for CPT4, HCPCS, and new CPT4_HCPCS in config/vocabularies.csv (current regexps miss 2-char modifiers and possibly other shapes); (3) handle the operator-name vs vocabulary_id vs internal-symbol namespace remap (entry.rb); (4) standardness/concept_ancestor implications of merged concepts. Punted now; do NOT lose it when next working in ConceptQL.

title: Review Dependabot PR: update sqlite3 requirement from ~1.3 to ~>2.9
description: Open Dependabot PR to update sqlite3 requirement. 4 days old. Review and merge or close.

title: Audit statement test coverage for all operators
description: There are 51 operator implementations and 280 statement fixtures across 60 categories. Audit which operators lack fixture coverage and add missing tests. Also verify gdm (non-wide) model is tested against DuckDB — currently only gdm_wide runs in CI.

title: Add just test recipe matching CI test suite
description: Create or update a justfile with a test recipe that runs the complete test suite for this project, matching what .github/workflows/ runs on CI. The goal: running just test locally passes if and only if CI will pass when the current commit is pushed to GitHub. Examine the CI workflow YAML to determine the exact test command, any setup steps (DB, env vars), and language version expectations. Use bundle exec for Ruby, uv run for Python, Rscript for R. Exit non-zero on any test failure.

title: Remove dead check_sequel code and fixture files
description: check_sequel in test/helper.rb is no longer called by any test after the gender/information_periods refactoring to inline regex assertions. The fixture files it reads in test/fixtures/ are also dead. Remove both.

title: Fix Presto adapter DISTINCT ON bug
description: Presto's adapter uses .distinct(:collection_id) in primary_concepts with a comment acknowledging it generates PostgreSQL-specific DISTINCT ON. Presto doesn't support DISTINCT ON. The DuckDB adapter correctly uses ROW_NUMBER instead. If Presto is ever exercised, it will fail at runtime. Should use the same ROW_NUMBER approach (which would come for free from finding 1: extracting into Generic).

title: Extract duplicated adapter methods into Generic base class
description: DuckDB#primary_concepts is identical to Spark#primary_concepts (ROW_NUMBER approach). DuckDB#days_between is identical to Postgres#days_between. The ROW_NUMBER approach should be the default in Generic, with Postgres overriding to use DISTINCT ON. This reduces duplication and makes adding future adapters easier.

title: Document CI DuckDB version derivation convention
description: The GitHub Actions workflow strips a trailing .0 from the gem version to derive the native library release tag (1.5.0.0 -> 1.5.0). If the gem versioning convention ever changes, this will silently download the wrong library. Add a comment in the workflow explaining the convention and why the .sub is needed.

title: Revisit supports_materialized? for DuckDB
description: DuckDB has supported AS MATERIALIZED in CTEs since v0.8.0, but the adapter returns false. This is safe but leaves potential query plan optimization on the table. Test whether enabling it works correctly and flip if so.

title: Consider expanding DuckDB CI to multiple data model configs
description: Postgres CI runs three data model configurations (gdm with gdm_vocabs, gdm with ohdsi_vocabs, gdm_wide). DuckDB runs only gdm_wide. Evaluate whether DuckDB should also run multiple configurations for parity, or document why gdm_wide alone is sufficient.

title: Add comment clarifying datediff argument ordering
description: The Ruby method datediff(from, to) returns from - to, not to - from. The DuckDB adapter correctly reverses args when calling the SQL function, but this is counterintuitive. A comment on the method signature convention would save future maintainers from confusion.

title: Add comment explaining CTE dependency detection heuristic
description: sort_extracted_ctes finds dependencies by checking if one CTE's SQL string contains another CTE's quoted name. This works because CTE names include PIDs and random hex making false positives essentially impossible, but a comment should explain why string matching is safe here.
